adapterBoundary.policy: Thin plugin, hook, MCP, and agent-host adapters can be the product boundary; keep them unless host registration proof says they are redundant.

gainHonesty.policy: Benchmark impact is not a current-repo savings claim without a matched baseline.

hardwareCalibration.policy: Hardware, sensors, clocks, timing, and physical devices need calibration or tuning proof before simplification.

oneRunnableCheck.policy: Non-trivial new logic should leave one smallest runnable check; trivial one-liners do not need ceremony.

requestedExplanation.policy: Explicitly requested reports, walkthroughs, phase notes, and rationale are not clutter.

shortcutDebt.policy: Intentional lean shortcuts need a ceiling and upgrade trigger.

evidence.snippet: thin adapter registering a host callback

findings.proofGuidance: Keep the adapter unless host registration proof shows it is unused and a fixture proves startup still wires the callback.
findings.recommendation: Do not delete thin host adapters from a less-code signal alone.

evidence.snippet: calibration offset for physical timing drift

findings.proofGuidance: Require before/after device or simulator timing evidence before simplifying calibration constants or timing branches.
findings.recommendation: Treat hardware calibration as proof-required product behavior, not clutter.

findings.proofGuidance: Keep requested explanation artifacts unless the requester or product docs prove the explanation is obsolete.
findings.recommendation: Do not remove explicit reports or walkthroughs just because they are prose.

findings.proofGuidance: Keep at least one focused runnable check for non-trivial adapter behavior before broader cleanup.
findings.recommendation: Do not delete the only cheap proof check for non-trivial behavior.

expectedAssertions: report-quality preserves the host-adapter protection question in JSON and Markdown
the fixture exposes behaviorGates in JSON and Behavior Gates in Markdown
the fixture separates adapter, hardware, requested-explanation, and one-check protection lanes
the fixture keeps scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly explicit
shareable output contains no local absolute paths or private app identifiers
fixtureCandidate.privateDataPolicy: This promoted fixture is synthetic. Use source reports only to choose report shape; do not copy private app code, local paths, screenshots, app-specific identifiers, or proprietary text.
fixtureCandidate.sourceQuestion: Does Lean Deck protect host adapters, hardware calibration, requested explanation, and one-check minimums from false less-code pressure?

actionGroups.firstExperiment: Leave the adapter and calibration files untouched, then collect proof that they are wired and behavior-bearing.
actionGroups.stopCondition: Stop if registration, startup, timing, or physical-device proof is missing.
actionGroups.validationRoute: Run the focused host-registration check and attach timing or calibration proof before any deletion attempt.

actionGroups.firstExperiment: Keep requested documentation and the one cheap behavior check until a replacement proof route exists.
actionGroups.stopCondition: Stop if the report was explicitly requested or the only focused runnable check would disappear.
actionGroups.validationRoute: Confirm requester intent and keep one smallest runnable check for non-trivial behavior.

blockedByProof.action: Do not delete or inline the host adapter without host registration proof.
blockedByProof.proofRequired: Attach startup or host-registration evidence showing the adapter is unused before removing it.

blockedByProof.action: Do not simplify hardware calibration without timing proof.
blockedByProof.proofRequired: Attach before/after timing or physical-device evidence.

keepList.reason: Thin adapters can be the product integration boundary.

keepList.proofRequired: Require timing or device calibration evidence before simplification.
keepList.reason: Hardware calibration can preserve physical-world behavior.

keepList.proofRequired: Require requester or doc-ownership evidence before removal.
keepList.reason: Explicitly requested explanation is product support, not generic clutter.

keepList.proofRequired: Keep one cheap runnable check unless equivalent proof exists.
keepList.reason: The one-check minimum protects non-trivial behavior from proofless cleanup.

topActions.action: Keep the host adapter until registration proof exists.

reportQualityQuestions: Does Lean Deck protect host adapters, hardware calibration, requested explanation, and one-check minimums from false less-code pressure?

scanScope.note: Synthetic fixture; no private source tree was scanned.

scopeBoundary.purpose: Synthetic public fixture for ShipGuard Lean Deck report-quality behavior.

leanDebtLedger.description: Intentional lean shortcuts found in comments. Every shortcut should name a ceiling and an upgrade trigger so deferrals stay reviewable.
